meta.note: Generated from PyPSA GitHub component metadata.

attributes.description: Unique name
Nominal voltage
Placeholder for bus type. Not implemented.
Longitude; the Spatial Reference System Identifier (SRID) is set in `n.srid`.
Latitude; the Spatial Reference System Identifier (SRID) is set in `n.srid`.
Carrier, such as "AC", "DC", "heat" or "gas".
Unit of the bus' carrier if the implicitly assumed unit ("MW") is inappropriate (e.g. "t/h"). Only descriptive. Does not influence any PyPSA functions.
Location of the bus. Does not influence the optimisation model but can be used for aggregation with `n.statistics`.
Voltage magnitude set point, per unit of `v_nom`.
Minimum desired voltage, per unit of `v_nom`. Placeholder attribute not currently used by any functions.
Maximum desired voltage, per unit of `v_nom`.  Placeholder attribute not currently used by any functions.
P,Q,V control strategy for power flow, must be "PQ", "PV" or "Slack". Note that this attribute is an output inherited from the controls of the generators attached to the bus. Do not set by hand.
Name of slack generator attached to slack bus.
Name of connected sub-network to which bus belongs. This attribute is set by `n.determine_network_topology()`. Do not set by hand.
active power at bus (positive if net generation at bus)
reactive power (positive if net generation at bus)
Voltage magnitude, per unit of `v_nom`
Voltage angle
Shadow price from energy balance constraint
Unique name
Emissions in tCO~2~ per MWh of primary energy (e.g. methane has 0.2 tCO~2~/MWh~thermal~).
Color for plotting (e.g. `matplotlib` [named colors](https://matplotlib.org/stable/gallery/color/named_colors.html) or hexadecimal color codes like #AB9812)
Descriptive name for statistics and visualisations (e.g. Natural Gas for carrier `gas`)
Maximum new installed capacity per investment period
Maximum capacity ratio for new installed capacity per investment period
Unique name
Name of bus to which generator is attached
P,Q,V control strategy for power flow, must be "PQ", "PV" or "Slack". Only relevant for "AC" and "DC" buses.
Placeholder for generator type. Not implemented.
Nominal power for limits  on `p` in optimization. Ignored if `p_nom_extendable=True`.
Nominal power of the generator module (e.g. fixed unit size of a nuclear power plant). Introduces integer variables if set.
Switch to allow capacity `p_nom` to be extended in optimization.
If `p_nom` is extendable in optimization, set its minimum value.
If `p_nom` is extendable in optimization, set its maximum value (e.g. limited by technical potential).
If `p_nom` is extendable in optimization, set its value.

The minimum output for each snapshot per unit of `p_nom` for the optimization (e.g. a minimal dispatch level for conventional power plants). Note that if `committable=False` and `p_min_pu>0`, this represents a must-run condition.

The maximum output for each snapshot per unit of `p_nom` for the optimization (e.g. changing availability of renewable generators due to weather conditions or a de-rating of conventional power plants).
Active power set point (for optimisation and power flow)
Active power used as a starting point for limiting the ramp in the very first snapshot (for optimisation)
The minimum total energy produced during a single optimization horizon.
The maximum total energy produced during a single optimization horizon.
Reactive power set point (for power flow)
Sign denoting the orientation of the dispatch variable (e.g. positive for generation, negative for consumption).
Prime mover energy carrier (e.g. coal, gas, wind, solar); required for global constraints on primary energy in optimisation
Marginal cost of production of 1 MWh.
Quadratic marginal cost of production of 1 MWh.
Whether to consider the component in optimization or not
Build year of the generator.
Lifetime of the generator.
Fixed period costs of extending `p_nom` by 1 MW (e.g. annuitized investment costs). Used directly in optimization unless `overnight_cost` is specified.
Overnight (upfront) investment cost per MW. If specified, PyPSA calculates annuity using `discount_rate` and `lifetime`. Takes precedence over `capital_cost`.
Discount rate for annuity calculation when using `overnight_cost`. Supports 0% rate (simple depreciation).
Fixed period operation and maintenance costs per MW, added to annuitized investment cost.
Ratio output and primary energy carrier input (e.g. 0.4 MWh~elec~/MWh~fuel~). This is required for global constraints on primary energy in optimization.
Apply unit commitment constraints.
Cost to start up the generator. Only used if `committable=True`.
Cost to shut down the generator. Only used if `committable=True`.
Stand-by cost for running the generator. This cost is incurred whenever the status is 1 (including when the dispatch decision is zero).
Minimum number of snapshots for status to be 1. Only used if `committable=True`. Does not consider snapshot weightings.
Minimum number of snapshots for status to be 0. Only used if `committable=True`. Does not consider snapshot weightings.
Number of snapshots that the generator was online before `n.snapshots` start. Only used if `committable=True` and `min_up_time>0`. Does not consider snapshot weightings.
Number of snapshots that the generator was offline before `n.snapshots` start. Only used if `committable=True` and `min_down_time>0`. Does not consider snapshot weightings.
Maximum active power increase from one snapshot to the next, per unit of the nominal power. Ignored if NaN. Does not consider snapshot weightings.
Maximum active power decrease from one snapshot to the next, per unit of the nominal power. Ignored if NaN. Does not consider snapshot weightings.
Maximum active power increase at start up, per unit of the nominal power. Only used if `committable=True`. Ignored if NaN.
Maximum active power decrease at shut down, per unit of the nominal power. Only used if `committable=True`. Ignored if NaN.
Weighting of a generator. Only used for network clustering.
Active power at bus (positive if net generation)
Reactive power (positive if net generation)
Optimised nominal capacity.
Status in the snapshot (1 is on, 0 is off). Only returned if `committable=True`.
Whether the unit was started in the snapshot (1 is yes, 0 is no). Only returned if `committable=True`.
Whether the unit was shut down in the snapshot (1 is yes, 0 is no).  Only returned if `committable=True`.
Shadow price of upper `p_nom` limit
Shadow price of lower `p_nom` limit
Shadow price of fixed power generation `p_set`
Shadow price of upper ramp up limit
Shadow price of lower ramp down limit
Unique name
Type of constraint (e.g. "primary energy", "tech_capacity_expansion_limit", "operational_limit", "transmission_volume_expansion_limit", "transmission_expansion_cost_limit")
Investment period for which the constraint is applied. If not specified, the constraint is applied to all investment periods together.
Bus to which the global constraint applies. Only relevant for `type="tech_expansion_limit". If not specified, the constraint applies to all buses.
If the global constraint is connected with a carrier, name the associated carrier attribute. This must appear as a column in `n.carriers`.
Constraint sense; must be one of <=, == or >=
Constant for right-hand-side of constraint for optimisation period. For a CO~2~ constraint, this would be tonnes of CO~2~.
Shadow price of global constraint
Unique name
Nominal frequency
Series resistance per length
Series reactance per length
Shunt capacitance per length
Nominal current
Can be "ol" for overhead line or "cs" for cable
Wire cross-section
References for electrical parameters
Unique name
Name of origin bus to which branch is attached.
Name of destination bus to which branch is attached.

Name of line standard type. If this is not an empty string "", the line standard type impedance parameters are multiplied with the `length` and divided/multiplied by `num_parallel` to compute `x`, `r`, etc. This will override any values set in `r`, `x, and `b`. If the string is empty, values manually provided for `r`, `x`, etc. are taken.

Series reactance, must be non-zero for AC branch for linearised power flow equations. If the line has series inductance $L$ in Henries then $x = 2\\pi f L$ where $f$ is the frequency in Hertz. Series impedance $z = r + jx$ must be non-zero for non-linear power flow calculations. Ignored if `type` defined.
Series resistance, must be non-zero for DC branch for linearised power flow equations. Series impedance $z = r + jx$ must be non-zero for the non-linear power flow. Ignored if `type` defined.
Shunt conductivity. Shunt admittance is $y = g + jb$.
Shunt susceptance. If the line has shunt capacitance $C$ in Farads then $b = 2\\pi f C$ where $f$ is the frequency in Hertz. Shunt admittance is $y = g + jb$. Ignored if `type` defined.
Limit of apparent power which can pass through branch in either direction. Ignored if `s_nom_extendable=True`.
Modular unit size of line expansion of `s_nom` (e.g. fixed rating of added circuit). Introduces integer variables.
Switch to allow capacity `s_nom` to be extended in optimisation.
If `s_nom_extendable=True`, set the minimum value of `s_nom_opt`.
If `s_nom_extendable=True`, set the maximum value of `s_nom_opt`.
If `s_nom` is extendable in optimization, set the value of `s_nom_opt`.

The maximum allowed absolute apparent power flow per unit of `s_nom` for the optimisation (e.g. can set `s_max_pu<1` to approximate $N-1$ contingency factor, or can be time-varying to represent weather-dependent dynamic line rating for overhead lines).
Fixed period costs of extending `s_nom` by 1 MVA (e.g. annuitized investment costs). Used directly unless `overnight_cost` is specified. Any `length` factor must already be included.
Overnight (upfront) investment cost per MVA. If specified, PyPSA calculates annuity using `discount_rate` and `lifetime`. Takes precedence over `capital_cost`.
Discount rate for annuity calculation when using `overnight_cost`. Supports 0% rate (simple depreciation).
Fixed period operation and maintenance cost per MVA, added to annuitized investment cost.
Whether to consider the component in optimisation or not
Build year of line.
Lifetime of line.
Length of line used when `type` is set. Also useful for calculating `capital_cost`.
Type of current. "AC" is the only valid value for lines.
Terrain factor for increasing `length` for `capital_cost` calculation.
When `type` is set, this is the number of parallel circuits. Can also be fractional. If `type` is empty "" this value is ignored.
Minimum voltage angle difference across the line.  Placeholder attribute not currently used.
Maximum voltage angle difference across the line. Placeholder attribute not currently used.
Name of sub-network to which lines belongs, as calculated by `n.determine_network_topology()`. Do not set by hand.
Active power at `bus0` (positive if branch is withdrawing power from `bus0`).
Reactive power at `bus0` (positive if branch is withdrawing power from `bus0`).
Active power at `bus1` (positive if branch is withdrawing power from `bus1`).
Reactive power at `bus1` (positive if branch is withdrawing power from `bus1`).
Per unit series reactance calculated by `n.calculate_dependent_values()` from `x` and `n.buses.v_nom`.
Per unit series resistance calculated by `n.calculate_dependent_values()` from `r` and `n.buses.v_nom`.
Per unit shunt conductivity calculated by `n.calculate_dependent_values()` from `g` and `n.buses.v_nom`.
Per unit shunt susceptance calculated by `n.calculate_dependent_values()` from `b` and `n.buses.v_nom`.
Effective per unit series reactance for linear power flow, calculated by `n.calculate_dependent_values()` from `x` and `n.buses.v_nom`
Effective per unit series resistance for linear power flow, calculated by `n.calculate_dependent_values()` from `r` and `n.buses.v_nom`
Optimised nominal capacity for apparent power.
Shadow price of lower `s_nom` limit $-F \\leq f$. Always non-negative.
Shadow price of upper `s_nom` limit $f \\leq F$. Always non-negative.
Unique name
Name of origin bus to which link is attached.
Name of first destination bus to which link is attached. Further destination buses (`bus2`, `bus3`, etc.) are automatically expanded as needed.
Placeholder for link type. Not implemented.
Carrier of the link describing its technology (e.g. gas boiler, electrolyser, HVDC link).

Efficiency of energy transfer from `bus0` to `bus1`. Can be time-dependent (e.g. to represent temperature-dependent heat pump COP).  Further efficiency attributes for further buses (e.g. `bus2`, `bus3`, etc.) are automatically expanded as needed (e.g. `efficiency2`, `efficiency3`, etc.).
Whether to consider the component in basic functionality or not
Build year
Lifetime
Limit of power which can pass through link (in units of `bus0`). Ignored if `p_nom_extendable=True`.
Unit size of link module (e.g. fixed blocks of 100 MW).
Switch to allow capacity `p_nom` to be extended.
If `p_nom_extendable=True`, set its minimum value.
If `p_nom_extendable=True`, set its maximum value.
If `p_nom` is extendable in optimization, set its value.
The dispatch set point for `p0` of the link (for optimisation and power flow).
Active power used as a starting point for limiting the ramp in the very first snapshot (for optimisation)
Minimal dispatch per unit of `p_nom` for the link. Can also be negative.
Maximal dispatch per unit of `p_nom` for the link. Can also be negative.
Fixed period costs of extending `p_nom` by 1 MW (unit of `bus0`, e.g. annuitized investment costs). Used directly unless `overnight_cost` is specified. Any `length` factor must already be included.
Overnight (upfront) investment cost per MW (unit of `bus0`). If specified, PyPSA calculates annuity using `discount_rate` and `lifetime`. Takes precedence over `capital_cost`.
Discount rate for annuity calculation when using `overnight_cost`. Supports 0% rate (simple depreciation).
Fixed period operation and maintenance costs per MW (unit of `bus0`), added to annuitized investment cost.
Marginal cost of 1 MWh consumption from `bus0` (e.g. including variable operation and maintenance costs of an electrolyser but excluding electricity costs).
Quadratic marginal cost for 1 MWh of consumption from `bus0`.
Stand-by cost for operating the link. This cost is incurred whenever the status is 1 (including when dispatch decision is zero).
Length of the link. Useful for calculating `capital_cost` for HVDC connections.
Terrain factor for increasing `capital_cost` calculated from `length`.
Apply unit commitment constraints.
Cost to start up the link. Only used if `committable=True`.
Cost to shut down the link. Only used if `committable=True`.
Minimum number of snapshots for status to be 1.  Only used if `committable=True`. Does not consider snapshot weightings.
Minimum number of snapshots for status to be 0.  Only used if `committable=True`. Does not consider snapshot weightings.
Number of snapshots that the link was online before network.snapshots start. Only read if `committable=True` and `min_up_time>0`. Does not consider snapshot weightings.
Number of snapshots that the link was offline before network.snapshots start. Only read if `committable=True` and `min_down_time>0`. Does not consider snapshot weightings.
Maximum increase from one snapshot to the next, per unit of `p_nom`. Ignored if NaN. Does not consider snapshot weightings.
Maximum decrease from one snapshot to the next, per unit of `p_nom`. Ignored if NaN. Does not consider snapshot weightings.
Maximum increase at start up, per unit of `p_nom`. Only used if `committable=True`. Ignored if NaN.
Maximum decrease at shut down, per unit of `p_nom`. Only used if `committable=True`. Ignored if NaN.

Delay of energy delivery to `bus1` in units of `n.snapshot_weightings.generators`. Energy withdrawn from `bus0` at snapshot `t` arrives at the first snapshot whose cumulative generator weighting start is at least `delay` units later. Auto-expanded as `delay2`, `delay3`, etc. for additional ports.

Whether delayed energy wraps cyclically from end to start of the optimization period. If False, energy is lost at the tail and first snapshots receive nothing from delayed links. Auto-expanded as `cyclic_delay2`, `cyclic_delay3`, etc. for additional ports.
Internal power (positive if link is withdrawing from `bus0`).
Power at `bus0` (positive if link is withdrawing from `bus0`).
Power at `bus1` (positive if link is withdrawing from `bus1`).
Optimised nominal capacity.
Status in the snapshot (1 is on, 0 is off). Only returned if `committable=True`.
Whether the unit was started in the snapshot (1 is yes, 0 is no). Only returned if `committable=True`.
Whether the unit was shut down in the snapshot (1 is yes, 0 is no).  Only returned if `committable=True`.
Shadow price of lower `p_nom` limit  $-F \\leq f$. Always non-negative.
Shadow price of upper `p_nom` limit $f \\leq F$. Always non-negative.
Shadow price of fixed dispatch `p_set`
Shadow price of upper ramp up limit
Shadow price of lower ramp down limit
Unique name
Name of bus to which load is attached.
Carrier of the load.
Placeholder for load type. Not implemented.
Active power consumption (positive if the load is consuming power).
Reactive power consumption (positive if the load is inductive).
Sign (opposite sign to generator)
Active power at bus (positive if net load)
Reactive power (positive if net load)
Whether to consider the component in optimisation or not.
Unique name
List of snapshots or time steps. All time-dependent series quantities are indexed by `network.snapshots`. To reset the snapshots, call `network.set_snapshots(new)`.

The weightings applied to each snapshot, so that snapshots can represent more than one hour or fractions of one hour. The objective weightings are used to weight snapshots in the objective function. The store weightings determine the state of charge change for stores and storage units. The generator weightings are used when calculating global constraints and energy balances.
Time periods of investment. Only used for multi investment optimisation. Years have to be integer and increasing (e.g. [2025,2030,2035]). Default is an empty pd.Index([]).

Weightings applied to each investment period. Objective weightings are multiplied with all cost coefficients in the objective function of the respective investment period (e.g. used to include social discount rate). Years weighting denote the elapsed time until the subsequent investment period (e.g. used for global constraints CO2 emissions).
The current snapshot/time/scenario, relevant e.g. when `network.pf()` is called without a snapshot argument.
Spatial Reference System Indentifier for x,y coordinates of buses. It defaults to standard longitude and latitude.
Coordinate Reference System for the Shape components. It defaults to standard longitude and latitude, given in srid.
All static bus information compiled by PyPSA from inputs. Index is bus names, columns are bus attributes.
All time-dependent bus information compiled by PyPSA from inputs. Dictionary keys are time-dependent series attributes, index is network.snapshots, columns are bus names.
All static line information compiled by PyPSA from inputs. Index is line names, columns are line attributes.
All time-dependent line information compiled by PyPSA from inputs. Dictionary keys are time-dependent series attributes, index is network.snapshots, columns are line names.
For each component type (buses, lines, etc.): static component information compiled by PyPSA from inputs. Index is component names, columns are component attributes.

For each component type (buses, lines, etc.): time-dependent component information compiled by PyPSA from inputs. Dictionary keys are time-dependent series attributes, index is network.snapshots, columns are component names.
Dynamically generated concatenation of branch DataFrames: network.lines, network.transformers and network.links. Note that this is a copy and therefore changing entries will NOT update the original.
Graph of network.
Unique name
Name of first bus to which process is attached.
Name of second bus to which process is attached. Further buses (`bus2`, `bus3`, etc.) are automatically expanded as needed.
Placeholder for process type. Not implemented.
Carrier of the process describing its technology (e.g. gas boiler, electrolyser, HVDC process).

Rate of energy transfer at `bus0` relative to internal power `p`. Can be time-dependent (e.g. to represent temperature-dependent heat pump COP).  `Rate`s for further buses (e.g. `bus2`, `bus3`, etc.) are automatically expanded as needed (e.g. `rate2`, `rate3`, etc.).

Rate of energy transfer at `bus1` relative to internal power `p`. Can be time-dependent (e.g. to represent temperature-dependent heat pump COP).  `Rate`s for further buses (e.g. `bus2`, `bus3`, etc.) are automatically expanded as needed (e.g. `rate2`, `rate3`, etc.).
Whether to consider the component in basic functionality or not
Build year
Lifetime
Limit of power which can pass through process (in units of `bus0`). Ignored if `p_nom_extendable=True`.
Unit size of process module (e.g. fixed blocks of 100 MW).

Overnight (upfront) investment cost per MW (unit of `bus0`). If specified, PyPSA calculates annuity using `discount_rate` and `lifetime`. Takes precedence over `capital_cost`.

Apply unit commitment constraints. This is only possible with `p_nom_extendable=False`.
Cost to start up the process. Only used if `committable=True`.
Cost to shut down the process. Only used if `committable=True`.
Minimum number of snapshots for status to be 1.  Only used if `committable=True`. Does not consider snapshot weightings.
Minimum number of snapshots for status to be 0.  Only used if `committable=True`. Does not consider snapshot weightings.
Number of snapshots that the process was online before network.snapshots start. Only read if `committable=True` and `min_up_time>0`. Does not consider snapshot weightings.
Number of snapshots that the process was offline before network.snapshots start. Only read if `committable=True` and `min_down_time>0`. Does not consider snapshot weightings.
Maximum increase from one snapshot to the next, per unit of `p_nom`. Ignored if NaN. Does not consider snapshot weightings.
Maximum decrease from one snapshot to the next, per unit of `p_nom`. Ignored if NaN. Does not consider snapshot weightings.
Maximum increase at start up, per unit of `p_nom`. Only used if `committable=True`. Ignored if NaN.
Maximum decrease at shut down, per unit of `p_nom`. Only used if `committable=True`. Ignored if NaN.

Delay of energy delivery to `bus0` in units of `n.snapshot_weightings.generators`. Energy withdrawn at snapshot `t` arrives at the first snapshot whose cumulative generator weighting start is at least `delay0` units later. Auto-expanded as `delay2`, `delay3`, etc. for additional ports.

Whether delayed energy wraps cyclically from end to start of the optimization period. If False, energy is lost at the tail and first snapshots receive nothing from delayed processes. Auto-expanded as `cyclic_delay2`, `cyclic_delay3`, etc. for additional ports.

Delay of energy delivery to `bus1` in units of `n.snapshot_weightings.generators`. Energy withdrawn at snapshot `t` arrives at the first snapshot whose cumulative generator weighting start is at least `delay1` units later. Auto-expanded as `delay2`, `delay3`, etc. for additional ports.

Whether delayed energy wraps cyclically from end to start of the optimization period. If False, energy is lost at the tail and first snapshots receive nothing from delayed processes. Auto-expanded as `cyclic_delay2`, `cyclic_delay3`, etc. for additional ports.
Power in component.
Power at `bus0` (positive if process is withdrawing from `bus0`).
Power at `bus1` (positive if process is withdrawing from `bus1`).
Optimised nominal capacity.
Status in the snapshot (1 is on, 0 is off). Only returned if `committable=True`.
Whether the unit was started in the snapshot (1 is yes, 0 is no). Only returned if `committable=True`.
Whether the unit was shut down in the snapshot (1 is yes, 0 is no).  Only returned if `committable=True`.

Calculated from `b` and `n.buses.v_nom`

Unique name
Name of bus to which storage unit is attached.
P,Q,V control strategy for PF, must be "PQ", "PV" or "Slack".

Nominal power for limits on `p` in optimisation. Ignored if `p_nom_extendable=True`.
Nominal power of the storage unit module. Introduces integer variables if set.
Switch to allow capacity `p_nom` to be extended in optimisation.

If `p_nom_extendable=True`, set the value of `p_nom_opt`.
The minimum output for each snapshot per unit of `p_nom` for the optimisation. Negative sign implies storing mode withdrawing power from bus.
The maximum output for each snapshot per unit of `p_nom` for the optimisation. Positive sign implies discharging mode injecting power into bus.
Active power set point. Sets p directly in power flow. In LOPF optimisation constrains net power (p_dispatch - p_store)
Reactive power set point (for power flow only)
Active power dispatch set point (for optimisation only)
Active power charging set point (for optimisation only)
Sign denoting the orientation of the dispatch variable.
Prime mover energy carrier (e.g. coal, gas, wind, solar); required for global constraints on primary energy in optimisation
Cost of spilling 1 MWh
Marginal cost of production (discharge) of 1 MWh.
Quadratic marginal cost of production (discharge) of 1 MWh.
Marginal cost of energy storage of 1 MWh for one hour.
Fixed period costs of extending `p_nom` by 1 MW (e.g. annuitized investment costs). Used directly unless `overnight_cost` is specified.
Overnight (upfront) investment cost per MW. If specified, PyPSA calculates annuity using `discount_rate` and `lifetime`. Takes precedence over `capital_cost`.
Discount rate for annuity calculation when using `overnight_cost`. Supports 0% rate (simple depreciation).
Fixed period operation and maintenance costs per MW, added to annuitized investment cost.
Whether to consider the component in optimisation or not
Build year
Lifetime
State of charge before the snapshots in the optimisation.
Switch: if True, the state of charge at the beginning of an investment period is set to `state_of_charge_initial`.
State of charge set points for snapshots in the optimisation.

Switch: if True, then `state_of_charge_initial` is ignored and the initial state of charge is set to the final state of charge for the group of snapshots in the optimisation (`soc[-1] = soc[len(snapshots)-1]`).
Switch: if True, the cyclic constraints are applied to each investment period separately.
Maximum state of charge capacity in terms of hours at full output power capacity `p_nom`
Efficiency of storage on the way into the storage.
Efficiency of storage on the way out of the storage.
Losses per hour to state of charge.
Inflow to the state of charge (e.g. due to river inflow in hydro reservoir).
Active power at bus (positive if net generation)
Active power dispatch at bus
Active power charging at bus
Reactive power (positive if net generation)
State of charge as calculated by the optimisation.
Spillage for each snapshot (e.g. hydro-dam letting water flow over the spillway without generating electricity).
Optimised nominal power.
Shadow price of upper p_nom limit
Shadow price of lower p_nom limit
Shadow price of fixed state of charge state_of_charge_set
Shadow price of storage consistency equations
Unique name
Name of bus to which store is attached.
Placeholder for store type. Not yet implemented.
Carrier of the store.
Nominal energy capacity (i.e. limit on `e`). Ignored if `e_nom_extendable=True`.
Nominal energy capacity of the store module. Introduces integer variables if set.
Switch to allow capacity `e_nom` to be extended in optimisation.
If `e_nom_extendable=True`, set the minimum value of `e_nom_opt`.
If `e_nom_extendable=True`, set the maximum value of `e_nom_opt`.
If `e_nom_extendable=True`, set the value of `e_nom_opt`.
Minimal value of `e` relative to `e_nom` for the optimisation.
Maximal value of `e` relative to `e_nom` for the optimisation.
Energy before the snapshots in the optimisation.
Switch: if True, then at the beginning of each investment period `e` is set to `e_initial`.
Switch: if True, then `e_initial` is ignored and the initial energy is set to the final energy for the group of snapshots in the optimisation.
Switch: if True, then the cyclic constraints are applied to each investment period separately.
Active power set point (for optimisation and power flow)
Reactive power set point (for power flow only)
Fixed energy filling level set point (for optimisation only)
Sign denoting orientation of the energy variable (`e`).
Marginal cost applied to both charging and discharging of 1 MWh.
Quadratic marginal cost of applied to charging and discharging of 1 MWh.
Marginal cost of energy storage of 1 MWh for one hour.
Fixed period costs of extending `e_nom` by 1 MWh (e.g. annuitized investment costs). Used directly unless `overnight_cost` is specified.
Overnight (upfront) investment cost per MWh. If specified, PyPSA calculates annuity using `discount_rate` and `lifetime`. Takes precedence over `capital_cost`.
Discount rate for annuity calculation when using `overnight_cost`. Supports 0% rate (simple depreciation).
Fixed period operation and maintenance costs per MWh, added to annuitized investment cost.
Losses per hour to energy level.
Whether to consider the component in optimisation or not
Build year
Lifetime
Power at bus (positive if net supply)
Reactive power (positive if net supply)
Energy level of store as calculated by the optimisation.
Optimised nominal energy capacity outputed by optimisation.
Shadow price of upper `e_nom` limit
Shadow price of lower `e_nom` limit
Shadow price of storage consistency equations (i.e. water values)
Unique name assigned based on order of sub-network in list of sub-networks.
Carrier determined from the buses in the sub-network.
Name of slack bus.
Unique name
Nominal frequency
Rated apparent power
Nominal voltage on high voltage side
Nominal voltage on low voltage side
Short circuit voltage
Real part of short circuit voltage
No load (open circuit) iron losses
No load (open circuit) current
Phase shift angle
Defines if tap changer is modelled at the primary 0 side (usually high-voltage) or the secondary 1 side (usually low voltage) (must be 0 or 1, defaults to 0)
Rated tap position, i.e. position at which the winding ratio corresponds to the ratio of the rated voltages
Minimum tap position
Maximum tap position
Tap step size in percentage of voltage change
References for electrical parameters
Unique name
Name of origin bus (typically higher voltage) to which transformer is attached.
Name of destination bus (typically lower voltage) to which transformer is attached.

Name of 2-winding transformer standard type. If this is not an empty string "", the transformer type impedance parameters are taken from the standard type along with `num_parallel`. This will override any values set in `r`, `x`, `g`, `b`, `s_nom`, `tap_ratio`, `tap_side` and `phase_shift`. If the string is empty, values manually provided for `r`, `x`, etc. are taken.
Model used for admittance matrix; can be "t" or "pi"; defaults to "t" following physics and DIgSILENT PowerFactory

Series reactance (per unit, using `s_nom` as base power); must be non-zero for AC branch in linear power flow. Series impedance $z = r + jx$ must be non-zero for the non-linear power flow. Ignored if type defined.

Series resistance (per unit, using `s_nom` as base power); must be non-zero for DC branch in linear power flow. Series impedance $z = r + jx$ must be non-zero for the non-linear power flow. Ignored if type defined.
Shunt conductivity (per unit, using `s_nom` as base power). Ignored if type defined.
Shunt susceptance (per unit, using `s_nom` as base power). Ignored if type defined.
Limit of apparent power which can pass through branch in either direction. Ignored if `s_nom_extendable=True`.
Modular unit size of transformer expansion of `s_nom`. Introduces integer variables.
Switch to allow capacity `s_nom` to be extended in optimisation.
If `s_nom_extendable=True`, set the minimum value of `s_nom_opt`.
If `s_nom_extendable=True`, set the maximum value of `s_nom_opt`.
If `s_nom` is extendable in optimization, set the value of `s_nom_opt`.
The maximum allowed absolute flow per unit of `s_nom` for the oprimisation.
Fixed period costs of extending `s_nom` by 1 MVA (e.g. annuitized investment costs). Used directly unless `overnight_cost` is specified.
Overnight (upfront) investment cost per MVA. If specified, PyPSA calculates annuity using `discount_rate` and `lifetime`. Takes precedence over `capital_cost`.
Discount rate for annuity calculation when using `overnight_cost`. Supports 0% rate (simple depreciation).
Fixed period operation and maintenance costs, added to annuitized investment cost.
When `type` is set, this is the number of parallel transformers (can also be fractional). If `type` is empty "" this value is ignored.
Ratio of per unit voltages at each bus for tap changed. Ignored if type defined.
Defines if tap changer is modelled at the primary 0 side (usually high-voltage) or the secondary 1 side (usually low voltage) (must be 0 or 1, defaults to 0). Ignored if type defined.
If the transformer has a `type`, determines position relative to the neutral tap position.
Voltage phase angle shift. Ignored if type defined.
Whether to consider the component in optimisation or not
Build year of transformer.
Lifetime of transformer.
Minimum voltage angle difference across the transformer.Placeholder attribute not currently used.
Maximum voltage angle difference across the transformer.Placeholder attribute not currently used.
Name of connected sub-network to which transformer belongs, as calculated by `n.determine_network_topology()`. Do not set by hand.
Active power at `bus0` (positive if branch is withdrawing power from `bus0`).
Reactive power at `bus0` (positive if branch is withdrawing power from `bus0`).
Active power at `bus1` (positive if branch is withdrawing power from `bus1`).
Reactive power at `bus1` (positive if branch is withdrawing power from `bus1`).
Per unit series reactance calculated by `n.calculate_dependent_values()` from `x` and `n.buses.v_nom`.
Per unit series resistance calculated by `n.calculate_dependent_values()` from `r` and `n.buses.v_nom`.
Per unit shunt conductivity calculated by `n.calculate_dependent_values()` from `g` and `n.buses.v_nom`.
Per unit shunt susceptance calculated by `n.calculate_dependent_values()` from `b` and `n.buses.v_nom`.
Effective per unit series reactance for linear power flow, calculated by `n.calculate_dependent_values()` from `x`, `tap_ratio` for transformers and `n.buses.v_nom`.
Effective per unit series resistance for linear power flow, calculated by `n.calculate_dependent_values()` from `x`, `tap_ratio` for transformers and `n.buses.v_nom`.
Optimised nominal capacity for apparent power.
Shadow price of lower `s_nom` limit. Always non-negative.
Shadow price of upper `s_nom` limit. Always non-negative.
Snapshot label or timestamp used by the workbook snapshot index.
